title: Campfire Nights
author: The Wanderers
content:
# Verse 1
[G]We built a fire under [Em]open skies
[C]Smoke curling up past the [D]tree line
[G]You passed the bottle and [Em]I closed my eyes
[C]Felt like the whole world was [D]finally fine

# Chorus
[C]Oh these campfire [G]nights
[Em]Keep burning [D]bright
[C]Won't somebody [G]play us a [D]song
[C]Till the morning [G]light
[Em]We'll all be al[D]right
[C]As long as we [G]sing along

# Verse 2
[G]Stars came out one by [Em]one by one
[C]Someone pulled out a [D]battered guitar
[G]We all knew the words [Em]before the night was done
[C]Funny how music [D]can carry you far

title: Morning Coffee
author: Elena Marsh
content:
# Verse 1
[C]Steam rising from the [Am]cup on the sill
[F]Sunlight crawling [G]slow across the floor
[C]You're still asleep and the [Am]whole world is still
[F]I don't need anything [G]more

# Pre-Chorus
[Am]These quiet hours [F]before the day begins
[Am]Are the ones I keep [G]coming back to

# Chorus
[F]Just morning [C]coffee and you
[G]That's all I ever [Am]needed
[F]Just morning [C]coffee and you
[G]Simple and [C]beautiful and true

# Verse 2
[C]Birds outside doing [Am]their usual thing
[F]Dog at my feet breathing [G]slow and deep
[C]I could stay here [Am]forever listening
[F]To the sound of you [G]in your sleep

title: River Road
author: Buck Holloway
content:
# Intro
// Slow, picked

# Verse 1
[D]Drove all night down the [A]river road
[G]Radio broke somewhere [D]past the state line
[D]Had a map but I [A]didn't need it
[G]Knew these curves since I [A]was nine

# Chorus
[G]River road, river [D]road
[A]Take me back where the [G]water flows
[D]River road, long and [A]winding
[G]That's the only home I've [D]known

# Verse 2
[D]Stopped at a diner in [A]some small town
[G]Waitress called me honey, [D]poured me some coffee
[D]Told her I was heading [A]back to the farm
[G]She said she'd never [A]leave this valley

# Bridge
[Bm]Some roads lead [G]you away
[A]Some roads bring you [D]home
[Bm]I always end up [G]here somehow
[A]No matter how far I [D]roam

title: Stargazer
author: Indigo Fall
content:
# Verse 1
[Am]Lying in the field [F]behind your house
[C]Counting satellites [G]drifting by
[Am]You said the universe [F]doesn't care about us
[C]I said maybe that's [G]why it's so beautiful

# Pre-Chorus
[F]We are small and we are [C]temporary
[Am]And somehow [G]that's okay

# Chorus
[F]Stargazer, [C]don't close your eyes
[Am]There's still so much [G]to see
[F]Stargazer, [C]hold on tonight
[Am]The dark is where we [G]find out who we'll [Am]be

# Verse 2
[Am]You traced a constellation [F]I didn't know
[C]Said your grandmother [G]taught you that one
[Am]Stories carried down through [F]generations
[C]Written in light from a [G]long dead sun

# Outro
[Am]Under the same [F]sky
[C]Under the same [G]sky

title: Last Train Home
author: The Hollow Pines
content:
# Verse 1
[Em]Platform empty at [C]half past ten
[G]I counted every [D]crack in the stone
[Em]Told myself I wouldn't [C]do this again
[G]Waiting on the last [D]train home

# Verse 2
[Em]You left a voicemail I [C]still haven't played
[G]The icon sits there like a [D]question mark
[Em]I've rehearsed every [C]word I would say
[G]But I can't find the nerve [D]after dark

# Chorus
[C]Last train home [G]take me somewhere
[D]Anywhere but [Em]here tonight
[C]Last train home [G]I don't care where
[D]Just get me back be[Em]fore first light

# Bridge
// Quiet, just vocals and guitar
[Em]Maybe tomorrow [C]I'll figure it out
[G]Maybe tomorrow [D]I'll call
[Em]Maybe this silence [C]is better than doubt
[G]Maybe nothing at [D]all

# Outro
[Em]Last train home [C]rolling away
[G]Carrying me back [D]to you [Em]
